verdict_reasoning: KLK2 is a classical secreted serine protease with signal peptide-directed ER-Golgi trafficking, canonically localized to the secretory pathway and extracellular space. However, recent publications (2025) document reproducible cell surface expression in prostate cancer cell lines (VCaP) and patient-derived tumors using FACS and confocal microscopy with EpCAM colocalization. This surface pool is targetable by clinical-stage bispecific T-cell engagers (pasritamig, Phase 1/3). The surface expression is documented in cancer tissue/cells, not baseline normal prostate epithelium, suggesting cancer/cell-state-induced ecto-pool. Meets criteria for contextual / cell_state_induced given documented, wash-resistant surface expression in a defined non-baseline condition (prostate cancer).
predicted_key_uncertainty: Unclear whether cell surface pool is truly cancer-restricted (cell_state_induced) vs. an underappreciated baseline surface biology of KLK2 only recently discovered. All evidence is from cancer tissue/cells; no data on normal prostate epithelium surface expression in recent literature. If baseline surface expression in normal prostate epithelium is confirmed, verdict should be 'yes' / 'classical_surface_receptor' or similar.
